title: Comic Book Hero Creator Born
story: Superheroes need creators too! William Messner-Loebs, the talented mind behind famous comic characters like the Flash and Wonder Woman, entered the world in Ferndale, Michigan. Born in 1949 on February 19, he grew up to become an American comics writer and artist, bringing exciting adventures to life on the page.
activity_question: What famous comic book characters did William Messner-Loebs work on?
follow_up_question: What other superhero would you create if you were a comic book artist?

title: Rock 'n' Roll's Hidden Hero
story: Alan Merrill rocked into the world in 1951. Born in The Bronx, New York City on February 19, this American singer and songwriter would leave a lasting mark on music. Merrill co-wrote the anthem 'I Love Rock 'n' Roll' with his band The Arrows. Though others made it famous, Alan's original tune became a timeless classic.
activity_question: What famous song did Alan Merrill co-write?
bonus_fact: Alan Merrill's birth name was Allan Sachs.

title: A Star Is Born: Gwen Taylor
story: Lights, camera, action! Gwen Taylor, a talented British actress, took her first bow on February 19, 1939. Born in the charming village of Crich, Derbyshire, England, Taylor would go on to shine in beloved shows like 'Heartbeat' and appear in the comedy classic 'Monty Python's Life of Brian'.
activity_question: In which village was Gwen Taylor born?
bonus_fact: Gwen Taylor played a character named Peggy in the popular TV series 'Heartbeat'.

title: Soul Singer Bobby Rogers Enters the World
story: Motown magic began when Bobby Rogers was born in Detroit in 1940. This future soul star would lend his smooth tenor voice to The Miracles, creating unforgettable hits. Rogers not only sang but also penned catchy tunes that had everyone grooving. His February 19 birthday marked the start of a musical journey that would shape pop history.
activity_question: What group did Bobby Rogers sing with?
bonus_fact: Bobby Rogers co-wrote 'The Way You Do the Things You Do,' a hit for another famous Motown group, The Temptations.

title: Skylab's Space Doctor Takes Off
story: Blast off! Joseph P. Kerwin rocketed into the world on February 19, 1932. Born in Oak Park, Illinois, he grew up to become a captain in the U.S. Navy Medical Corps and an astronaut. Kerwin's big adventure came when he joined the Skylab 2 mission, bringing his medical skills to the stars!
activity_question: What was Joseph P. Kerwin's profession before becoming an astronaut?
activity_choices: Captain in the U.S. Navy Medical Corps
activity_answer: Captain in the U.S. Navy Medical Corps
follow_up_question: What special skills do you think a space doctor needs?

title: Sprinting to Olympic Gold
story: Leslie Laing raced into history! Born in Linstead, Jamaica in 1925, he became a lightning-fast runner. On February 19, years later, Laing helped Jamaica sprint to victory. He was part of the incredible team that won gold in the men's 4x400m relay at the 1952 Olympics. Their amazing time set a new world record, showing just how speedy they were!
activity_question: What event did Leslie Laing win gold in at the Olympics?
follow_up_question: Can you name the country Leslie Laing represented in the Olympics?
